# Research - 2026-07-20

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> Monday, `date -u +%u` = 1. Weekly repos (claude-code, aider) polled per
> cadence rule. Aider shipped expanded Claude model support (#5173: opus-4-5,
> opus-4-6-20260205, opus-4-7-20260416, claude-3-7-sonnet), bash tree-sitter
> tags for repomap (#5132), and gpt-5.5 model settings. Claude-code shows
> only automated changelog updates (no actionable commits). Cline continues
> from yesterday's team spawning, optional peer deps, and API credit
> detection. Kilocode shows minor commits (custom file extensions #12306
> already analyzed yesterday, CI test stabilization #12311, other items
> already covered). OpenCode shows UI polish, context overflow patterns, and
> minor fixes. Zero open `auto-implement` issues (unchanged). Most new
> commits are releases, model catalog updates, or internal plumbing; one
> actionable feature (bash tree-sitter tags #5132) is applicable to Alexi but
> MODERATE priority (requires evaluating Alexi's current repomap approach).

## Executive Summary

- **#1 (MODERATE, ~5h, tool)**: Aider #5132 added bash tree-sitter tags for
  repomap support. Aider's repomap (repository map) generates a compressed
  file+function outline for large codebases to fit context. Previously bash
  scripts were indexed as opaque files; now the tree-sitter query extracts
  function definitions (`name() { ... }`, `function name { ... }`) so they
  appear in the map. Port to Alexi: check if Alexi implements repomap /
  codebase indexing. Alexi has `codesearch`, `glob`, `grep`, `definitions`,
  `recall` tools; `definitions` uses tree-sitter for symbol extraction.
  Review `src/tool/tools/definitions.ts` to see if it supports bash and if
  it feeds a repomap-like feature. If Alexi has repomap indexing, add bash
  tree-sitter queries (query file at
  https://github.com/aider-ai/aider/blob/main/aider/queries/tree-sitter-bash-tags.scm).
  Estimated ~5h: review current definitions/repomap implementation (~1.5h),
  port bash tree-sitter query if applicable (~2h), test on bash scripts
  (~1h), update docs (~30m). MODERATE value (bash scripts are common in CI /
  agent workflows, better symbol extraction aids context compression).
- **#2 (LOW, ~2h, provider)**: Aider #5173 expanded `ANTHROPIC_MODELS` list
  with recent Claude model names (opus-4-5, opus-4-6-20260205,
  opus-4-7-20260416, claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219). This list maps bare Claude
  names to the `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env var for sanity checks. Port to Alexi:
  Alexi uses SAP AI Core deployments, not direct Anthropic API keys, so the
  exact key-mapping use case differs. The principle (keep a curated list of
  known model names for validation / default selection) is applicable. Review
  `src/providers/model-match.ts` and `src/providers/index.ts` to see if
  Alexi maintains a known-model list for validation. If yes, add the new
  Claude 4.x family names. If no (model IDs are freeform / derived from SAP
  deployments), this is not applicable. Low value (edge case, user specifies
  model explicitly via `-m` flag or routing config; validation is helpful but
  not critical). Estimated ~2h: review current model validation (~1h), add
  new model names if applicable (~30m), test (~30m).
- **#3 (LOW, ~1h, tool)**: OpenCode #37840 expanded context overflow patterns
  to detect additional provider-specific overflow messages. Previously the
  CLI detected only a few hardcoded strings; now it matches more patterns
  (\"context window\", \"maximum context\", \"too_many_tokens\", etc.). Port to
  Alexi: review `src/core/orchestrator.ts` and `src/providers/` for context
  overflow detection. If Alexi parses provider errors for overflow signals,
  add the expanded patterns from OpenCode #37840. Low value (error
  presentation improvement, most users already see overflow messages from
  provider HTTP status). Estimated ~1h: review current error handling (~30m),
  add expanded patterns (~20m), test (~10m).
- **#4 (SKIP, ~gpt-5.5)**: Aider added gpt-5.5 model settings across
  providers. Alexi uses SAP AI Core, not direct OpenAI. GPT-5.5 support
  depends on SAP AI Core deploying that model (not under Alexi's control).
  Skip.
- **Rotation**: kilocode 2 dryness (zero new actionable items today, 2 < 5,
  keep daily), cline 1 dryness (yesterday's items already analyzed), opencode
  2 dryness (zero new actionable items today, 2 < 5, keep daily), aider 0
  dryness (active, two items analyzed: bash tree-sitter tags, expanded
  ANTHROPIC_MODELS). claude-code 1 dryness (automated changelog only, first
  weekly poll). No demotions.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Bash tree-sitter tags for repomap (tool, ~5h, MODERATE)

- **What**: Aider #5132 added bash tree-sitter tags for repomap support.
  Aider's repomap (repository map) generates a compressed file+function
  outline for large codebases, allowing the model to navigate a 1000-file
  repo within context budget. Previously bash scripts were indexed as opaque
  files (file path only, no function signatures). The PR adds a tree-sitter
  query for bash (`aider/queries/tree-sitter-bash-tags.scm`) that extracts
  function definitions: POSIX-style `name() { ... }` and Bash-style
  `function name { ... }`. Functions now appear in the repomap as
  `script.sh:42 function deploy_app` instead of just `script.sh`. The query
  captures `function_definition` nodes and extracts the function name from
  the `word` child (POSIX) or `name: (word)` field (Bash keyword form). The
  implementation follows the existing pattern for Python, JavaScript, etc.:
  one `.scm` query file per language, loaded by the tree-sitter tags module.
- **Why**: Alexi has multiple tools that involve code navigation and symbol
  extraction: `codesearch` (semantic search), `definitions` (tree-sitter
  symbol extraction), `grep` (text search), `recall` (local session search).
  The `definitions` tool (`src/tool/tools/definitions.ts`) uses tree-sitter
  to extract symbols from source files (inferred from the tool list and
  common aider-like architecture). Port to Alexi: check if `definitions.ts`
  supports bash and if it feeds a repomap-like context compression feature.
  If yes, add bash tree-sitter queries following the pattern from Aider
  #5132 (query file:
  https://github.com/aider-ai/aider/blob/main/aider/queries/tree-sitter-bash-tags.scm).
  The query extracts `function_definition` nodes and captures the name. If
  Alexi does not have repomap (definitions is used only for on-demand symbol
  lookup, not context compression), the value is lower but still applicable:
  better bash function extraction improves the definitions tool's output for
  shell scripts. MODERATE value (bash scripts are common in CI workflows,
  GitHub Actions composite steps, agent harness scripts; better symbol
  extraction aids navigation and context compression). Estimated ~5h: review
  current definitions tool implementation (~1.5h), port bash tree-sitter
  query if applicable (~2h: add query file, wire into definitions parser,
  handle POSIX vs Bash syntax), test on bash scripts from `.github/` or
  examples (~1h), update docs (~30m).
- **Source**: aider-ai/aider PR #5132 \"feat: add bash tree-sitter tags for
  repomap support\" (merged 2026-07-20T03:12Z, commit `7f8a9e1`). Query file
  at
  https://github.com/aider-ai/aider/blob/main/aider/queries/tree-sitter-bash-tags.scm.
  Full PR body at https://github.com/aider-ai/aider/pull/5132.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: moderate (~5h: review definitions tool, port
  bash tree-sitter query, test, update docs).
- **Status**: MODERATE (bash scripts are common in agent workflows; improves
  context compression and symbol extraction for definitions tool).

#### 2. Expand ANTHROPIC_MODELS list with recent Claude names (provider, ~2h, LOW)

- **What**: Aider #5173 expanded the `ANTHROPIC_MODELS` list in
  `aider/models.py` to include recent Claude model names:
  `claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219`, `claude-opus-4-1`,
  `claude-opus-4-1-20250805`, `claude-opus-4-5`, `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`,
  `claude-opus-4-6-20260205`, `claude-opus-4-7-20260416`. This list is used
  by `sanity_check_models` to map bare Claude model names (no provider
  prefix) to the `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` environment variable; models not in this
  list fall through to the keymap provider lookup. The list was stale (had
  3.x, 3-5, sonnet-4, opus-4, plus 4.5/4.6/4.7 unrounded names) but was
  missing the dated variants and 3-7-sonnet. The PR brings it up to date with
  `aider/resources/model-settings.yml`. Part of Aider's direct-API-key model
  access (user provides `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, Aider validates the model name
  against the known list).
- **Why**: Alexi uses SAP AI Core deployments, not direct Anthropic API keys,
  so the exact key-mapping use case differs. SAP AI Core proxies multiple
  providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Mistral, etc.) via deployment IDs; model IDs
  are freeform strings like `sap-ai-core/anthropic--claude-4.7-opus`
  (inferred from `AGENTS.md` model pins). The principle (keep a curated list
  of known model names for validation / default selection) is applicable if
  Alexi performs model-name validation. Port to Alexi: review
  `src/providers/model-match.ts` and `src/providers/index.ts` to see if
  Alexi maintains a known-model list for validation (e.g. to warn the user
  \"model X is not recognized\" or to map short aliases to full IDs). If yes,
  add the new Claude 4.x family names (opus-4-5, opus-4-6, opus-4-7,
  sonnet-3-7). If no (model IDs are freeform / derived from SAP deployments
  at runtime, no hardcoded list), this is not applicable. Low value (edge
  case; user specifies model explicitly via `-m` flag or routing config;
  validation is helpful but not critical; SAP AI Core likely surfaces
  \"deployment not found\" errors at request time). Estimated ~2h: review
  current model validation logic (~1h), add new model names to known list if
  applicable (~30m), test with a new model ID (~30m).
- **Source**: aider-ai/aider PR #5173 \"Expand ANTHROPIC_MODELS list with
  recent Claude model names\" (merged 2026-07-20T02:48Z, commit `9c7a1f3`).
  Full PR body at https://github.com/aider-ai/aider/pull/5173.
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: small (~2h: review model validation, add
  new names if applicable, test).
- **Status**: LOW (edge case; Alexi uses SAP AI Core deployments, not direct
  Anthropic keys; validation is helpful but not critical; confirm current
  behavior before implementing).

#### 3. Expand context overflow patterns (tool, ~1h, LOW)

- **What**: OpenCode #37840 expanded context overflow patterns to detect
  additional provider-specific overflow messages. Previously the CLI detected
  only a few hardcoded strings (\"context_length_exceeded\", \"maximum context
  length\"); now it matches more patterns: \"context window\", \"maximum
  context\", \"too_many_tokens\", \"context limit\", \"exceeds the context\",
  case-insensitive. Part of OpenCode's error classification (classify
  provider errors as overflow vs. auth vs. rate limit vs. generic network
  failure, then display a clear user-facing message). The fix improves error
  presentation: instead of showing the raw provider error, the CLI detects
  overflow and suggests \"Reduce context size or use a model with a larger
  window\".
- **Why**: Alexi has error handling in `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts`
  and `src/core/orchestrator.ts` (inferred from provider structure). Review
  to see if Alexi parses provider errors for overflow signals. SAP AI Core
  likely returns HTTP 400 or 413 for context overflow (inferred from standard
  LLM API patterns). The principle (classify provider-specific overflow
  messages and surface them clearly) is applicable. Port to Alexi: if Alexi
  parses error messages for overflow detection, add the expanded patterns
  from OpenCode #37840 (\"context window\", \"maximum context\",
  \"too_many_tokens\", \"context limit\", \"exceeds the context\"). Low value
  (error presentation improvement; most users already see overflow messages
  from provider HTTP status; expanding patterns catches edge cases where
  status is 400 but message text differs). Estimated ~1h: review current
  error handling (~30m), add expanded patterns if applicable (~20m), test
  with mock overflow response (~10m).
- **Source**: sst/opencode PR #37840 \"fix(llm): expand context overflow
  patterns\" (merged 2026-07-20T04:27Z, commit `3a8b9f2`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/sst/opencode/pull/37840.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~1h: review error handling, add
  expanded patterns, test).
- **Status**: LOW (edge case; error presentation improvement; most overflow
  cases already surfaced by HTTP status; confirm current behavior before
  implementing).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Priority order**: One MODERATE item (#1 bash tree-sitter tags), two LOW
   items (#2 expanded ANTHROPIC_MODELS, #3 context overflow patterns), one
   SKIP (#4 gpt-5.5). #1 is the highest-value item today: bash scripts are
   common in agent workflows (GitHub Actions, CI, composite steps), and
   better symbol extraction improves context compression and definitions tool
   output.
2. **Bash tree-sitter tags (#1)**: Surface as an issue if Alexi implements
   repomap / codebase indexing or if the definitions tool supports bash.
   Check `src/tool/tools/definitions.ts` for bash support and repomap usage.
   If applicable, port bash tree-sitter query from Aider #5132
   (https://github.com/aider-ai/aider/blob/main/aider/queries/tree-sitter-bash-tags.scm).
   Estimated ~5h: review definitions tool (~1.5h), port query (~2h), test
   (~1h), update docs (~30m). MODERATE value.
3. **Expanded ANTHROPIC_MODELS (#2)**: Do NOT surface as an issue unless
   Alexi performs model-name validation. Review `src/providers/model-match.ts`
   and `src/providers/index.ts` to see if a known-model list exists. If yes,
   add opus-4-5, opus-4-6, opus-4-7, sonnet-3-7. If no (freeform model IDs),
   skip. Reference: Aider #5173. Estimated ~2h if needed. LOW value.
4. **Context overflow patterns (#3)**: Do NOT surface as an issue unless
   Alexi's error handling actively parses overflow messages. Review
   `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts` and `src/core/orchestrator.ts` for
   overflow detection. If present, add expanded patterns from OpenCode #37840
   (\"context window\", \"maximum context\", \"too_many_tokens\", \"context
   limit\", \"exceeds the context\"). Reference: OpenCode #37840. Estimated
   ~1h if needed. LOW value.
5. **GPT-5.5 (#4)**: Do NOT surface as an issue. Alexi uses SAP AI Core; GPT
   model availability depends on SAP deployments. Skip.
6. **Rotation**: kilocode 2 dryness (zero new actionable items today; 2 < 5,

   Next weekly poll: Monday 2026-07-27. No demotions.
7. **Observation**: Zero open `auto-implement` issues (unchanged). The
   engineering pipeline remains clear. Today's scan produced one MODERATE

8. **Carry-forward from yesterday**: Yesterday's brief (2026-07-19) surfaced
   zero actionable issues (all LOW or SKIP). The day before (2026-07-18)
   identified two actionable items: #1 (custom file extensions, MODERATE,
